title: Pre-Award — Budget Development
content:
Comprehensive guidance for preparing the budget (also called the cost proposal) for a Morgan State University sponsored project. ORA emphasizes that the budget is equally as important as the technical proposal. For questions contact ask.ora@morgan.edu or 443-885-4044 (fax 443-885-8280).

Budget overview: Budgets are comprehensive planning documents integrating details for achieving proposal objectives, accounting for both long-range goals and short-term targets. A budget typically includes direct costs, other direct costs, indirect costs, and other budget elements.

Expense categories:

Personnel salaries and wages: senior personnel, post-doctoral researchers, lab technicians, graduate/undergraduate students, teaching assistants, and clerical staff.

Fringe benefits (current rates): Regular full-time/non-contractual employees (year round) = 42% of gross salary; Faculty during academic year = 42%; Faculty during summer = 9%; Contractual employees (year round) = 9%. See the Fringe Benefit Rate page for the official rate letter.

Equipment: defined as property with acquisition cost of $5,000 or more and an expected service life exceeding one year. Brief descriptions and justifications are required.

Travel: includes domestic and international travel for fieldwork and conferences. Current 2025 reimbursable rates under the State of Maryland include personal automobile mileage at $0.70 per mile, and meal per diems of $15 (breakfast), $18 (lunch), and $30 (dinner) for a total of $63 daily. GSA Per Diem Rates and the MSU University Travel page are also referenced.

Participant support: costs that defray expenses for students in training activities, conferences, or symposiums. Participants must be non-MSU employees receiving services (not providing them). Morgan employees do not qualify as participants, and indirect costs are not allowed on participant support.

Materials and supplies: supplies, printing, instructional materials, and audiovisual/computer supplies.

Consultant services: require descriptions of proposed services, time estimates, and payment rates. MSU faculty may not be listed as consultants.

Subawards (also called subgrants, subagreements, or subcontracts): require clear budget provisions, separate identification, and a service explanation. See the Pre-Award Subawards page.

Facilities and Administrative (F&A) / Indirect Cost: Morgan State's currently negotiated rate for Organized Research (On-Campus) is 53%-54% of modified total direct cost (see the F&A Cost Rates page for the full rate schedule by fiscal year and activity type).

Linked templates and resources from this page: PI Handbook 2 (Grant Budgets) PDF, NSF Budget Template (protected Excel), Budget Justification Template (Word), and Budget Preparation PowerPoint Slides PDF.

key_facts.participant_support: MSU employees do not qualify; no indirect costs on participant support
